Click a highlighted state to jump to its race card.

Alaska Senate

General election 2026-11-03

Cook: Toss Up Crystal Ball: Toss-up
DS

Dan Sullivan (R)

Incumbent US Senator

Background

Two-term senator seeking a third term; running on his committee seniority and national security credentials, while his campaign has been complicated by a voter-confusion controversy involving a second candidate with the same name. (source)

Current storyline

The Sullivan race has been dominated by a controversy over a same-named challenger, Dan J. Sullivan of Petersburg, whose late-filed candidacy Republicans and the incumbent's campaign allege was designed to confuse voters and siphon support to help Democrat Mary Peltola. Alaska's Division of Elections initially disqualified the challenger, but courts—first a Superior Court judge and then the Alaska Supreme Court—ordered him reinstated on the primary ballot, while the NRSC filed an FEC complaint alleging coordination between the challenger and a Democratic consultant. NBC News has since reported that the FBI, the Alaska attorney general's office, and the U.S. attorney's office are investigating whether the challenger's campaign was part of a conspiracy to confuse voters, though Peltola's campaign has denied any involvement. The episode has unfolded alongside a broader, closely watched contest between Sullivan and Peltola that both parties view as pivotal to Senate control in 2026.

source · source · source

Endorsements

President Donald TrumpSen. Lisa MurkowskiNational Republican Senatorial CommitteeAlaska Republican PartyANCSA Regional AssociationAmericans for Prosperity Action

Committee assignments

Committee on Armed ServicesCommittee on Commerce, Science, and TransportationCommittee on Environment and Public WorksCommittee on Veterans' Affairs
MP

Mary Peltola (D)

Background

Former U.S. Representative and the first Alaska Native elected to Congress, narrowly unseated in 2024; running an "affordability" campaign focused on the cost of living and rural/fisheries issues as one of Democrats' top recruits nationally. (source)

Current storyline

The race has been defined by its unusual competitiveness for a heavily Republican-leaning state, with polling showing the contest within a few points throughout the year. A late-June/early-July New York Times/Siena poll found Sullivan leading Peltola 47%-45% among likely voters, a result that contradicted Peltola campaign messaging and differed from Alaska Survey Research polls showing Peltola with a roughly five-point lead. The race has also been shaped by Sen. Lisa Murkowski's decision to back Sullivan despite having endorsed Peltola in the past and describing her as a long-time friend, saying she believes keeping a Republican Senate majority is in Alaska's best interest. Additional coverage has focused on a separate ballot controversy involving a like-named Republican challenger, Dan J. Sullivan, whom Sen. Sullivan's campaign called a 'sham candidate' after he was initially removed from and then restored to the ballot by courts.

source · source · source

Endorsements

EMILYs ListEnd Citizens UnitedSEIU 775Alaska Democratic Party (Chair Eric Croft)

Recent news

2026-07-04 FBI and Alaska state and federal prosecutors investigate whether the second Dan Sullivan's campaign was recruited to confuse voters and benefit Peltola; both his campaign and Peltola's deny coordination.
2026-07-01 A New York Times/Siena poll shows Sullivan narrowly ahead of Peltola 47%-45%, contradicting recent messaging from the Peltola campaign that it was leading.
2026-06-29 Alaska Supreme Court rules that a second candidate also named Dan Sullivan is eligible to run, after a lower court fight over his removal from the ballot.

Alabama Senate

General election 2026-11-03

Cook: Solid Republican Crystal Ball: Safe R
BM

Barry Moore (R)

U.S. Representative (AL-2)

Background

U.S. Representative running for the open seat after incumbent Tommy Tuberville opted to run for governor instead of seeking re-election; running as a Trump-aligned conservative. (source)

Current storyline

Running to fill Tuberville's seat as he vacates it for governor; has campaigned as a staunch Trump ally in a state Democrats haven't carried statewide since 2008.

Running a longshot campaign in one of the most Republican states in the country.

General election 2026-11-03

Cook: Solid Republican Crystal Ball: Safe R
TC

Tom Cotton (R)

Incumbent US Senator

Background

Two-term incumbent senator and Army veteran; won renomination with over 81% of the primary vote, running as a reliably conservative voice on national security and China policy. (source)

Current storyline

A prominent national security hawk often mentioned as a potential future presidential contender; faces no serious primary or general election threat.

Current storyline

A rice farmer running to give Democrats a foothold in a state they haven't won at the Senate level since 2002.

Florida Senate

General election 2026-11-03

Cook: Likely Republican Crystal Ball: Likely R
AM

Ashley Moody (R)

Incumbent U.S. Senator

Background

In office since 2025, appointed by Gov. DeSantis to fill the seat vacated by Marco Rubio; previously Florida Attorney General. Has Trump's endorsement and no major primary challenger. Primary not yet held (Aug 18, 2026). (source)

Current storyline

Cook shifted this race from Safe to Likely R after Vindman's entry and fundraising haul; Moody hasn't faced Florida voters since her appointment.

Endorsements

None on file

AV

Alex Vindman (D)

Retired Army Lt. Colonel

Background

Retired Army lieutenant colonel and national security whistleblower whose testimony featured in Trump's first impeachment; consolidated the Democratic field after raising $1.7M in his first 24 hours as a candidate. Primary not yet held (Aug 18, 2026). (source)

Current storyline

His campaign launch drew national attention and record-breaking small-dollar fundraising, though analysts still see Florida as having moved too far right for Democrats to seriously contest without major outside investment.

Georgia Senate

General election 2026-11-03

Cook: Lean Democrat Crystal Ball: Leans Democratic
JO

Jon Ossoff (D)

Incumbent US Senator

Background

Two-term-seeking incumbent senator who won his first term by just over a point in a 2021 runoff; running on a fundraising advantage and a message contrasting himself with Trump-aligned Republicans, amid growing speculation about a future presidential bid. (source)

Current storyline

Ossoff enters the general election with a significant financial and polling advantage, leading Collins by 13 points (56%-43%) in a late-June Fox News poll, while Cook Political Report has moved the race from Toss-up to Lean Democrat as his standing has strengthened. He has also drawn national attention for pointed criticism of the Trump administration, fueling speculation about a potential 2028 presidential run that he has publicly downplayed.

source · source

Endorsements

SPLC Action Fund

Committee assignments

Committee on AppropriationsCommittee on Rules and AdministrationSelect Committee on Intelligence
MC

Mike Collins (R)

US Representative (GA-10)

Background

U.S. Representative for Georgia's 10th District since 2023 and self-described 'MAGA trucker'; running as an America First loyalist after winning a competitive GOP runoff with a last-minute Trump endorsement. (source)

Current storyline

Collins enters the general election as an underdog after winning a bruising three-way GOP primary, defeating former football coach Derek Dooley in a June 16 runoff on the strength of a last-minute Trump endorsement that overcame Gov. Brian Kemp's backing of Dooley. Recent polling shows him trailing Ossoff by double digits, and his campaign is expected to lean heavily on national Republican outside spending to close the gap.

source · source

Endorsements

President Donald TrumpClub for Growth PACNational Border Patrol Council

Recent news

2026-06-16 Mike Collins wins the Republican runoff over Derek Dooley with about 55% of the vote after a last-minute Trump endorsement, setting up the November matchup with Ossoff.
2026-06-16 Cook Political Report moves its rating of the race from Toss-up to Lean Democrat, citing Ossoff's fundraising and polling strength.

Iowa Senate

General election 2026-11-03

Cook: Lean Republican Crystal Ball: Leans Republican
AH

Ashley Hinson (R)

U.S. Representative (IA-2)

Background

Three-term U.S. Representative for Iowa's 2nd District and former television news anchor; running as a Trump-endorsed Republican emphasizing her record on agriculture and opposition to Democratic economic proposals. (source)

Current storyline

Hinson entered the general election with a substantial fundraising advantage and endorsements from Trump, Gov. Kim Reynolds, and retiring Sen. Joni Ernst, but has struggled with net-negative favorability tied to declining approval of Trump and economic fallout from tariffs and the Iran war. Recent polling is unusually close for a state Trump carried by 13 points in 2024 -- a Fox News poll found Turek leading by 4 points -- prompting both Cook Political Report and Sabato's Crystal Ball to downgrade the race from Likely to Lean Republican.

source · source

Endorsements

President Donald TrumpGov. Kim ReynoldsSen. Joni Ernst
JT

Josh Turek (D)

Iowa State Representative

Background

Iowa state representative and two-time Paralympic wheelchair basketball gold medalist; running on healthcare and agricultural economy issues after winning a hard-fought Democratic primary, aiming to end nearly two decades without a Democratic Senate win in Iowa. (source)

Current storyline

Turek has outperformed expectations, benefiting from outside spending by the Democratic-aligned VoteVets and a political environment that has turned unexpectedly favorable as Iowans blame Republicans for tariff-driven price increases and the Iran War's economic fallout. Multiple polls, including a Fox News survey showing him ahead 50%-46%, have shown him competitive or leading -- a historic shift in a state Democrats haven't won a Senate race in since 2008.

source · source

Endorsements

None on file

Recent news

2026-06-27 A Fox News poll shows Turek leading Hinson 50%-46%, positioning Iowa as one of the most competitive Senate races in the country despite Trump's 13-point 2024 win there.
2026-06-03 Cook Political Report and Sabato's Crystal Ball both downgrade the race from Likely to Lean Republican, citing a shifting political environment and Turek's strength as a candidate.
2026-06-02 Turek and Hinson win their respective primaries, with Hinson taking 73.9% over Jim Carlin and Turek defeating Zach Wahls 62.6%-37.4%, setting up the general election to replace retiring Sen. Joni Ernst.

Maine Senate

General election 2026-11-03

Cook: Toss Up Crystal Ball: Toss-up
SC

Susan Collins (R)

Incumbent US Senator

Background

Five-term senator and chair of the Senate Appropriations Committee; running on her seniority and a record of delivering federal funding for Maine rather than her decades in Washington. (source)

Current storyline

The defining story in the race has shifted from Collins herself to the turmoil in the Democratic field: nominee Graham Platner, who won the June 9 primary after Gov. Janet Mills suspended her campaign, is facing a new sexual assault allegation that he denies, prompting numerous Democratic leaders and groups, including VoteVets and the Working Families Party, to rescind endorsements and call on him to withdraw. Collins has largely stayed above the fray while touting her seniority and Appropriations Committee chairmanship, and has received supportive comments from President Trump, who has said he would campaign for her. Coverage from the Washington Post, Axios, CNBC, and the Associated Press has focused heavily on whether Platner will remain in the race and who might replace him if he exits, given Maine's status as one of the most competitive Senate contests of the cycle.

source · source · source

Endorsements

Donald TrumpNational Republican Senatorial CommitteeRepublican National Committee

Committee assignments

Committee on Appropriations (Chairman)Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and PensionsSelect Committee on Intelligence
TJ

Troy Jackson (D)

Former Maine Senate President

Background

Former Maine Senate President and fifth-generation logger; entered the race after original nominee Graham Platner withdrew on July 10, 2026, following a rape allegation, which Platner denies. Bellows, Shah, and Kleban have all since dropped out and endorsed Jackson, who enters the July 25 nominating convention with majority delegate support already secured. (source)

Current storyline

Went from one of several replacement candidates to the clear frontrunner within about two weeks, consolidating the field ahead of Saturday's convention; recent polling shows him running competitively against Collins.

source

Endorsements

None on file

Recent news

2026-07-27 Deadline for the Maine Democratic Party to name a replacement nominee, if Platner has withdrawn.
2026-07-13 Legal deadline for Platner to withdraw from the race under Maine law.
2026-07-08 Shenna Bellows says she would "seriously consider" entering the race if Platner withdraws; Nirav Shah also signals interest.

Market includes several stale primary-era names; showing the three that matter -- the two actual nominees plus the leading potential replacement given the active withdrawal deadline.

Recent polling

North Carolina Senate

General election 2026-11-03

Cook: Lean Democrat Crystal Ball: Leans Democratic
RC

Roy Cooper (D)

Former Governor of North Carolina

Background

Two-term former North Carolina governor undefeated in six statewide races since 2000; running on his crossover appeal and pragmatic-moderate brand to try to end nearly two decades without a Democratic Senate win in the state. (source)

Current storyline

Cooper has built a consistent double-digit polling lead in most surveys, prompting Cook Political Report and Sabato's Crystal Ball to both move the race from Toss-up to Lean Democrat this spring, and prediction markets now give him roughly an 85-87% chance of winning. His campaign has focused on affordability and cost-of-living messaging while leaning on his statewide name recognition from six prior winning campaigns.

source · source

Endorsements

None on file

MW

Michael Whatley (R)

Former RNC Chairman

Background

Former Republican National Committee chairman making his first bid for elected office; running as a Trump-aligned 'America First' Republican after securing the nomination with the president's immediate endorsement. (source)

Current storyline

Whatley continues to struggle with name recognition -- one poll found 44% of voters simply unfamiliar with him -- despite a fundraising infrastructure built around his RNC chairmanship and an early Trump endorsement. Analysts note that while Cooper has never lost a statewide race, he has underperformed his polling in past contests, leaving Whatley's campaign hopeful the race tightens as it nationalizes.

source · source

Endorsements

President Donald Trump

Recent news

2026-06-01 Sabato's Crystal Ball also moves the race to Leans Democratic, citing Cooper's double-digit polling advantage.
2026-04-13 Cook Political Report shifts its rating of the race from Toss-up to Lean Democrat, citing Cooper's consistent polling lead -- the same day it made an identical move in Georgia.
2026-03-03 Whatley wins the GOP primary with 64.6% of the vote after receiving Trump's endorsement, while Cooper wins the Democratic nomination with minimal opposition.

Ohio Senate

General election 2026-11-03

Cook: Toss Up Crystal Ball: Toss-up
JH

Jon Husted (R)

Incumbent US Senator (appointed)

Background

Appointed to the Senate in January 2025 after serving as Ohio's lieutenant governor and secretary of state; running with Trump's endorsement on his record of economic development and job-training initiatives. (source)

Current storyline

Husted, running to keep the seat he was appointed to, faces Democratic former Senator Sherrod Brown in a race widely rated a toss-up despite Ohio's recent Republican lean. Husted secured President Trump's early endorsement and has emphasized ties to Trump in his campaign advertising, while also touting several labor union endorsements he flipped from Brown's 2024 coalition. Public polling has been volatile and conflicting, with some surveys (Fox News, AARP) showing Brown ahead by several points and others (New York Times/Siena, Emerson) showing Husted with a narrow lead, underscoring how competitive the contest has become. In March 2026, Husted also testified for the defense in the FirstEnergy bribery scandal criminal trial, describing a 2018 dinner meeting with then governor-elect DeWine and FirstEnergy executives later indicted in the $60 million bribery scheme.

source · source · source

Endorsements

President Donald TrumpOhio Republican PartyNFIB (National Federation of Independent Business)Susan B. Anthony Pro-Life AmericaSentinel Action Fund

Committee assignments

Committee on AppropriationsCommittee on Environment and Public WorksCommittee on Health, Education, Labor, and PensionsCommittee on Small Business and EntrepreneurshipSpecial Committee on Aging
SB

Sherrod Brown (D)

Former US Senator

Background

Three-term former senator attempting a comeback after his 2024 defeat; running on affordability, health care costs, and his long-standing 'fighting for workers' brand that helped him win statewide in a red-trending state for decades. (source)

Current storyline

Brown, attempting a political comeback after his 2024 loss, is running against Republican incumbent Jon Husted (appointed to the seat after JD Vance became vice president) in a race both parties see as pivotal to Senate control. The campaign has centered on affordability and health care, with Brown attacking Husted over ACA subsidy votes and rising prices while Husted blames Brown's 32-year Washington record for current problems. In June 2026, both campaigns ran dueling ads accusing each other's donors of ties to Jeffrey Epstein associates, a story fact-checked by PolitiFact. Brown has maintained a fundraising advantage, reporting far more cash on hand than Husted, even as the two campaigns and outside groups are expected to spend record sums in what is being called one of the most expensive Senate races in the country.

source · source · source

Endorsements

Ohio AFL-CIOOhio Federation of Teachers (OFT)United SteelworkersActors' Equity AssociationChuck Schumer (Senate Minority Leader, recruited Brown to run)

Recent news

2026-05-05 Sherrod Brown wins the Democratic nomination unopposed by a serious challenger, setting up the general election matchup against Husted.
2026-05-05 Both campaigns debut their first TV ads; Brown's ad ties Husted to campaign donations linked to Jeffrey Epstein associate Leslie Wexner.
2026-04-24 Cook Political Report moves the race from "Leans Republican" to "Toss Up" as a GOP-aligned super PAC announces plans to spend \9 million defending the seat.

$9,419,311
Brown

CLEARPATH ACTION FUND, INC. Husted support $92,660

Special election to fill the seat left vacant by the sudden death of Sen. Lindsey Graham in July 2026. Texas Senate

General election 2026-11-03

Cook: Lean Republican Crystal Ball: Leans Republican
KP

Ken Paxton (R)

Former Texas Attorney General

Background

Texas Attorney General from 2015 until resigning in January 2025 to run for Senate, twice impeached-and-acquitted and under a long-unresolved federal securities fraud indictment; running as the most Trump-aligned MAGA candidate after ousting four-term incumbent Sen. John Cornyn in a 28-point primary runoff blowout. (source)

Current storyline

Paxton enters the general election as a genuinely vulnerable nominee -- an unusual position for a Texas Republican in a state Trump carried by 14 points -- with multiple post-runoff polls showing him trailing Talarico, a stark reversal from Cornyn's earlier polling advantage. His candidacy carries real baggage, including his indictment, 2023 impeachment, and a public divorce, all of which Talarico has featured heavily in early general election messaging, while Paxton leans on Trump's endorsement and attacks on Talarico's cultural positions.

source · source

Endorsements

President Donald Trump
JT

James Talarico (D)

Texas State Representative

Background

Austin-area state representative and former public school teacher; running an economic-populist campaign focused on affordability after winning his primary outright, now positioned as Democrats' strongest Texas Senate candidate in a generation. (source)

Current storyline

Talarico has emerged as an unusually strong general election contender, leading or narrowly trailing Paxton in most post-runoff polling and maintaining a significant fundraising advantage over the scandal-plagued Republican nominee. Trump has personally attacked Talarico on Truth Social, calling him among the worst candidates he's seen, while Talarico has centered his campaign on cost-of-living concerns and cast the race as a referendum on Paxton's corruption record rather than traditional partisan lines.

source · source

Endorsements

None on file

Recent news

2026-05-30 A post-runoff poll shows Talarico leading Paxton 47%-44%, with nearly a third of Cornyn's primary voters saying they will back the Democrat in November.
2026-05-27 Cook Political Report and Sabato's Crystal Ball both downgrade the race from Likely to Lean Republican immediately after Paxton's win, citing his legal and political baggage.
2026-05-26 Ken Paxton defeats four-term incumbent Sen. John Cornyn by 28 points in the Republican primary runoff after a late Trump endorsement -- the first time a sitting Texas senator has lost a primary since 1970.

General election 2026-11-03

Cook: Solid Democrat Crystal Ball: Safe D
MW

Mark Warner (D)

Incumbent U.S. Senator

Background

In office since 2009; a former Virginia Governor, running for a fourth term. He is the only Democrat who qualified for the primary ballot. Primary technically not yet held (Aug 4, 2026). (source)

Current storyline

A centrist voice known for bipartisan dealmaking, particularly on tech and intelligence policy; faces no primary opposition.

Retired Army major general; the fundraising leader in a three-way Republican primary. Primary not yet held (Aug 4, 2026). (source)

Current storyline

Leading a three-way Republican primary on fundraising, positioning himself as a national security-focused challenger.

Current storyline

The first woman elected to represent West Virginia in the Senate, running for a third term with no serious opposition.

U.S. Representative (WY-AL)

Current storyline

Best known for defeating Liz Cheney in the 2022 House primary; now the clear favorite for this open seat with Lummis's backing.

Alaska Governor

General election 2026-11-03

Cook: Likely Republican Crystal Ball: Likely R
BW

Bernadette Wilson (R)

Businesswoman

Background

Businesswoman; emerged as an early frontrunner in polling of the crowded 11-candidate Republican field. (source)

Current storyline

Alaska uses a top-four open primary (Aug 18) followed by a ranked-choice general election, so this genuinely crowded field (17 filed candidates, 11 Republicans, 3 Democrats, 3 independents) for this open seat won't be resolved into a clear two-way matchup until after the primary. Recent polling shows Wilson, former state Sen. Tom Begich (D), former Anchorage Mayor Dave Bronson (R), and former state Sen. Click Bishop (R) as the current top tier.

source

Endorsements

None on file

TB

Tom Begich (D)

Former Alaska State Senator

Background

Former Alaska state senator; the leading declared Democrat, though former U.S. Rep. Mary Peltola has also signaled interest in entering the race. (source)

Current storyline

One of only three Democrats in a Republican-dominated field; incumbent Gov. Mike Dunleavy is term-limited, leaving this seat open for the first time in years.

Endorsements

None on file

U.S. Senator

Background

U.S. Senator since 2021 and former Auburn football coach; gave up his Senate seat to run for this open seat, vacated by term-limited Gov. Kay Ivey. (source)

Current storyline

Won the Republican nomination with Trump's endorsement; a rematch of the 2020 Senate race he won against Doug Jones.

None on file

Doug Jones (D)

Former U.S. Senator

Background

Former U.S. Senator (2018-2021), best known for prosecuting the 1963 16th Street Baptist Church bombing case; running as the Democratic nominee. (source)

Current storyline

A rematch of the 2020 Senate race, which Tuberville won; Democrats haven't won a gubernatorial election in Alabama since 1998.

General election 2026-11-03

Cook: Lean Democrat Crystal Ball: Lean D
KH

Katie Hobbs (D)

Incumbent Governor

Background

Elected in 2022, narrowly defeating Kari Lake by about 17,000 votes; previously Arizona Secretary of State and a state legislator. (source)

Current storyline

Running for a second term with a fundraising advantage over her Republican opponent and relatively strong approval ratings; Cook moved this race from Toss-up to Lean D in July 2026.

Endorsements

None on file

AB

Andy Biggs (R)

U.S. Representative (AZ-5)

Background

U.S. Representative since 2017 and former Arizona Senate President; won the Republican primary decisively over fellow Rep. David Schweikert. (source)

Current storyline

A close Trump ally and House Freedom Caucus member who secured Trump's endorsement and backing from Turning Point USA to win a competitive primary; now faces the test of whether his hardline profile can win a swing-state general election.

California Governor

General election 2026-11-03

Cook: Solid Democrat Crystal Ball: Safe D
XB

Xavier Becerra (D)

Former U.S. HHS Secretary

Background

Former U.S. Secretary of Health and Human Services under Biden and former California Attorney General; surged late in a crowded primary field after Rep. Eric Swalwell dropped out. (source)

Current storyline

Advanced to the general election in California's top-two primary system; a genuine Republican making the general is notable given the GOP hasn't won a statewide race in California since 2006.

Endorsements

None on file

SH

Steve Hilton (R)

Fox News Commentator

Background

Fox News commentator and former adviser to UK Prime Minister David Cameron; advanced to the general election, one of the few Republicans to do so statewide in recent California history. (source)

Current storyline

Running on affordability and cost-of-living, framing his campaign around making California "work again" for the middle class; faces a steep uphill climb given the state's deep Democratic lean.

General election 2026-11-03

Cook: Toss Up Crystal Ball: Toss-up
KL

Keisha Lance Bottoms (D)

Former Atlanta Mayor

Background

Mayor of Atlanta from 2018-2022 and a former Biden administration official; won the Democratic nomination outright, avoiding a runoff, in a crowded field. (source)

Current storyline

The clear Democratic frontrunner throughout the primary, she now faces a Republican nominee who pulled off one of the biggest upsets in recent Georgia political history.

Endorsements

None on file

RJ

Rick Jackson (R)

Healthcare Executive

Background

A wealthy healthcare business owner and first-time candidate who self-funded over $100 million into his campaign. (source)

Current storyline

Pulled off a stunning upset in the June 16 runoff, defeating Trump-endorsed Lt. Gov. Burt Jones despite Trump's repeated public backing of his opponent -- one of the most expensive gubernatorial primaries in Georgia history.

Joe Lombardo (R)

Incumbent Governor

Background

Elected in 2022, defeating incumbent Gov. Steve Sisolak; a former Clark County Sheriff. (source)

Current storyline

Running for a second term with strong approval ratings, in a state Trump won in 2024 for the first time since 2004.

Endorsements

None on file

AF

Aaron Ford (D)

Nevada Attorney General

Background

Nevada Attorney General since 2019 and former Nevada Senate Majority Leader; considered the clear Democratic frontrunner in a less crowded field than other battleground states. (source)

Current storyline

Running on his record as Attorney General pursuing opioid settlements and consumer protection cases, positioning himself as an experienced alternative to Gov. Lombardo.

Endorsements

None on file

New York Governor

General election 2026-11-03

Cook: Solid Democrat Crystal Ball: Safe D
KH

Kathy Hochul (D)

Incumbent Governor

Background

Governor since 2021, having succeeded Andrew Cuomo; the first woman to serve as Governor of New York, running for a second full term. (source)

Current storyline

Has focused on public safety, subway crime, and affordability, while navigating a challenging relationship with New York City progressives and managing migrant-related costs.

Ohio Governor

General election 2026-11-03

Cook: Toss Up Crystal Ball: Toss-up
AA

Amy Acton (D)

Former Ohio Health Director

Background

Physician who served as Ohio's Health Director under Gov. Mike DeWine during the COVID-19 pandemic; ran unopposed for the Democratic nomination. (source)

Current storyline

Running as a bipartisan-appeal figure against her former boss's chosen successor, drawing contrasts with her opponent's wealth.

Endorsements

None on file

VR

Vivek Ramaswamy (R)

Biotech Entrepreneur

Background

Biotech billionaire and 2024 Republican presidential primary candidate, briefly co-led the Department of Government Efficiency; won the Republican nomination easily with endorsements from both Trump and outgoing Gov. Mike DeWine. (source)

Current storyline

One of the most nationally-watched gubernatorial candidates of the cycle given his presidential-campaign profile; Cook moved this race from Lean R to Toss-up in July 2026.

General election 2026-11-03

TK

Tina Kotek (D)

Incumbent Governor

Background

Elected in 2022, narrowly defeating Christine Drazan by 3.4 points; previously Speaker of the Oregon House. (source)

Current storyline

Running for a second term facing a rematch against the same Republican opponent she narrowly beat in 2022; Cook moved this race from Solid to Likely D in July 2026, citing her middling approval ratings.

Endorsements

None on file

CD

Christine Drazan (R)

Former Oregon House Minority Leader

Background

A former Oregon state representative and House Minority Leader; narrowly lost to Kotek in the 2022 general election. (source)

Current storyline

Running a rematch against Kotek, hoping a shift in the political environment gives her the edge she narrowly missed in 2022.

California 1st District

Mike McGuire (D)

California State Senate President Pro Tem since 2022, previously a Sonoma County Supervisor; running in this newly-redrawn, now Democratic-leaning district after redistricting added Santa Rosa.

James Gallagher (R) (inc.)

In office since 2026 after winning a special election; previously served as California Assembly Republican Leader and a state assemblyman since 2014; now running in a district redrawn to favor Democrats.

California 2nd District

Jared Huffman (D) (inc.)

In office since 2013; previously a California state assemblyman and water/environmental attorney representing the North Coast.

Robin Littau (R)

Enterprise School Board Member, small business owner, and U.S. Coast Guard veteran.

California 3rd District

Ami Bera (D) (inc.)

In office since 2013; a practicing physician and former chief medical officer for Sacramento County before Congress.

Robb Tucker (R)

Nevada County Supervisor and businessman.

California 4th District

Mike Thompson (D) (inc.)

In office since 1999; an Army veteran and Napa County Supervisor before Congress, now the dean of California's House delegation.

Jimih L. Jones (R)

Republican nominee running in a wine-country district Democrats have comfortably held for over two decades.

California 5th District

Tom McClintock (R) (inc.)

In office since 2009; previously a longtime California state legislator and 2003 gubernatorial recall candidate.

Michael Masuda (D)

Democratic nominee running in a Sierra Nevada foothills district Republicans have held safely for years.

California 6th District

Kevin Kiley (R) (inc.)

In office since 2023; a former California state assemblyman and 2021 gubernatorial recall candidate, representing one of California's more competitive Republican-held seats.

Richard Pan (D)

Pediatrician and former California state senator known for vaccine-related legislation, running as the Democratic nominee.

California 7th District

Doris Matsui (D) (inc.)

In office since 2005, succeeding her late husband Rep. Bob Matsui; previously a Clinton White House official.

Charles Hoelter (R)

Republican nominee running in a Sacramento district Democrats have held for two decades.

California 8th District

John Garamendi (D) (inc.)

In office since 2009; previously California's Lieutenant Governor and the state's first elected Insurance Commissioner.

Rudy Recile (R)

Businessman and retired Army officer; a repeat candidate for this seat in 2022 and 2024.

California 9th District

Josh Harder (D) (inc.)

In office since 2019; a venture capitalist and teacher before entering politics, representing the Central Valley.

John McBride (R)

Strength and conditioning coach; a repeat candidate for this seat in 2024.

California 10th District

Mark DeSaulnier (D) (inc.)

In office since 2015; previously a California state senator and Contra Costa County Supervisor.

Jeffrey Frese (R)

Businessman.

California 11th District

Scott Wiener (D)

California state senator since 2016 and former San Francisco supervisor, running for this open seat vacated by retiring Speaker Emerita Nancy Pelosi.

David Ganezer (R)

Republican nominee running in this heavily Democratic San Francisco seat.

California 12th District

Lateefah Simon (D) (inc.)

In office since 2025; previously a BART Board Director and civil rights advocate, a MacArthur "Genius Grant" recipient.

Stephen Slauson (R)

Republican nominee running in a heavily Democratic East Bay/Oakland district.

California 14th District

Melissa Hernandez (D)

Democratic nominee for this East Bay seat, part of a crowded primary field that included incumbent Eric Swalwell running elsewhere this cycle.

Wendy Huang (R)

Republican nominee running in a heavily Democratic East Bay district.

California 15th District

Kevin Mullin (D) (inc.)

In office since 2023; previously a California state assemblyman and South San Francisco mayor.

Charles Hoelter (R)

Republican nominee running on the San Francisco Peninsula, a heavily Democratic area.

California 16th District

Sam Liccardo (D) (inc.)

In office since 2025; previously the Mayor of San Jose from 2015 to 2023.

Peter Soule (R)

Retired businessman and Republican activist.

California 17th District

Ro Khanna (D) (inc.)

In office since 2017; an attorney and former Commerce Department official, now a prominent progressive voice representing Silicon Valley.

Ritesh Tandon (R)

Businessman and computer engineer; a frequent candidate for this seat.

California 18th District

Zoe Lofgren (D) (inc.)

In office since 1995; a former San Jose city council member and one of the most senior members of the California delegation.

Shane Lewis (R)

Electrical test engineer; ran for school board in 2024.

California 19th District

Jimmy Panetta (D) (inc.)

In office since 2017; a former Monterey County prosecutor and Navy veteran, son of former CIA Director and Defense Secretary Leon Panetta.

Peter Coe Verbica (R)

Republican nominee running on the Central Coast, a Democratic-leaning area.

California 24th District

Salud Carbajal (D) (inc.)

In office since 2017; a former Santa Barbara County Supervisor and Marine Corps veteran.

Robert Bob J. Smith (R)

Republican nominee running in the Santa Barbara-based Central Coast district.

California 25th District

Raul Ruiz (D) (inc.)

In office since 2013; an emergency room physician with degrees from Harvard, representing the Coachella Valley.

Joe Males (R)

Hemet City Councilman, businessman, and Marine Corps veteran.

California 28th District

Judy Chu (D) (inc.)

In office since 2009; a former California Board of Equalization member and psychologist, the first Chinese-American woman elected to Congress.

April Verlato (R)

Republican nominee running in the San Gabriel Valley, a longtime Democratic stronghold.

California 29th District

Luz Rivas (D) (inc.)

In office since 2023; previously a California state assemblywoman and electrical engineer, representing the San Fernando Valley.

Rudy Melendez (R)

Republican nominee running in a heavily Democratic San Fernando Valley district.

California 30th District

Laura Friedman (D) (inc.)

In office since 2023; previously a California state assemblywoman and Glendale city council member.

Scott Alan Meyers (R)

Commercial director.

California 31st District

Gilbert Cisneros (D) (inc.)

In office since 2023; previously Under Secretary of Defense for Personnel and Readiness and a Navy veteran.

Eric J. Ching (R)

Former Walnut Mayor and businessman; a repeat candidate for this seat in 2022 and 2024.

California 41st District

Linda Sánchez (D)

Currently represents CA-38 (in office since 2003); running in this newly-redrawn, now safely Democratic district after redistricting. Rated a Democratic pickup by Crystal Ball (not yet reflected on Cook).

Mitch Clemmons (R)

Republican nominee in a district redistricting turned solidly Democratic.

California 32nd District

Brad Sherman (D) (inc.)

In office since 1997; previously a tax attorney and CPA, representing the San Fernando Valley.

Larry Thompson (R)

Attorney and talent manager; a repeat candidate for this seat in 2020 and 2024.

California 33rd District

Pete Aguilar (D) (inc.)

In office since 2015; previously the Mayor of Redlands, now House Democratic Caucus Chair.

Stephanie M. Vargas (R)

Colton Chief Deputy City Clerk.

California 34th District

Jimmy Gomez (D) (inc.)

In office since 2017; previously a California state assemblyman, representing downtown Los Angeles.

Calvin Lee (R)

Republican nominee running in a heavily Democratic Los Angeles district.

California 35th District

Norma Torres (D) (inc.)

In office since 2015; previously a California state senator and Mayor of Pomona.

Mike Cargile (R)

Independent filmmaker and Army veteran; a frequent candidate for this seat.

California 36th District

Ted Lieu (D) (inc.)

In office since 2015; previously a California state senator and Air Force JAG officer, representing the South Bay/Westside.

Houston Brignano (R)

Tech executive and Iraq War veteran.

California 37th District

Sydney Kamlager-Dove (D) (inc.)

In office since 2023; previously a California state senator, representing South LA/Culver City.

Baltazar Fedalizo (R)

Republican nominee running in a heavily Democratic Los Angeles district.

California 38th District

Hilda Solis (D)

Los Angeles County Supervisor and former U.S. Labor Secretary under President Obama; running for this open seat.

Pedro Antonio Casas (R)

Psychologist and retired Army colonel.

California 39th District

Mark Takano (D) (inc.)

In office since 2013; a former teacher and school board member, representing Riverside.

Steve Manos (R)

Lake Elsinore City Councilman and realtor.

California 42nd District

Robert Garcia (D) (inc.)

In office since 2023; previously the Mayor of Long Beach, representing the Long Beach area.

Arizona 6th District

General election 2026-11-03

Cook: Toss Up Crystal Ball: Toss-up
JC

Juan Ciscomani (R)

Incumbent U.S. Representative

Background

Two-term incumbent born in Hermosillo, Mexico, and raised in Tucson; a former senior adviser to Gov. Doug Ducey, considered a moderate Republican voice on the state's most competitive swing district. (source)

Current storyline

Seeking a third term in one of the nation's most competitive districts (Cook PVI: EVEN); was significantly outraised by his Democratic challenger in early 2026 fundraising, a warning sign given how close his past two elections have been.

source

Endorsements

None on file

JM

JoAnna Mendoza (D)

Retired U.S. Marine

Background

Retired U.S. Marine who served 20 years including combat tours in Iraq and Afghanistan; grew up in a farmworker family in rural Arizona and previously served as a congressional staffer for AZ-01. (source)

Current storyline

Emerged as the strongest Democratic recruit in this race, significantly outraising the Republican incumbent in early 2026 ($2.44M to $1.14M); backed by EMILY's List and the DCCC's Red to Blue program.

source · source

Endorsements

EMILY's ListDCCC Red to Blue

Cash on hand

Ciscomani
$4,135,163
Mendoza
$3,260,752

Total spent

Freshman Democrat who flipped this Central Valley seat in 2024 by a razor-thin margin; running for a second term in one of the most competitive districts in California. (source)

Won this seat by just 187 votes in 2024, the closest House race in the country that cycle; running for a second term in a district Trump narrowly carried.

California 22nd District

General election 2026-11-03

Cook: Toss Up Crystal Ball: Toss-up
DV

David Valadao (R)

Incumbent U.S. Representative

Background

Six-term Republican and dairy farmer representing California's Central Valley; one of the few Republicans who voted to impeach Trump in 2021, running on a moderate, independent-minded brand in a heavily Latino, competitive district. (source)

Current storyline

Valadao has developed a reputation for outperforming the top of the ticket in this heavily Latino, competitive district, but new district lines from 2025 redistricting make his path narrower. Villegas advanced through a top-two primary field that included state Assemblymember Jasmeet Bains, with both major forecasters continuing to rate the race a Toss-up.

source · source

Endorsements

None on file

RV

Randy Villegas (D)

Visalia School Board Trustee

Background

Political science professor at College of the Sequoias and Visalia school board member; advanced from a crowded top-two primary running as an economic populist, framing the race as a fight for "the soul of the Democratic Party." (source)

Current storyline

Villegas advanced from the June 2 top-two primary ahead of state Assemblymember Jasmeet Bains, positioning himself as the champion of the party's working-class wing against a Valadao campaign built on independence from national Republicans.

source

Colorado 8th District

General election 2026-11-03

Cook: Toss Up Crystal Ball: Toss Up
GE

Gabe Evans (R)

Incumbent U.S. Representative

Background

Freshman Republican and former Army Black Hawk pilot and police officer; flipped this seat by less than 2,500 votes in 2024 and is seeking a second term in one of the closest districts in the country. (source)

Current storyline

Colorado's 8th District has flipped between parties in each of its two elections since being created in 2021, with neither winner topping 50% of the vote. Rutinel enters the general election backed by labor unions and former Interior Secretary Ken Salazar after defeating Bird in the Democratic primary, setting up a continuation of the district's core competitive dynamic against freshman Rep. Evans.

source · source

Endorsements

None on file

MR

Manny Rutinel (D)

Colorado State Representative

Background

State representative and economist who worked with the Army Corps of Engineers; won a competitive Democratic primary over former state Rep. Shannon Bird, running on opposition to the Trump administration and healthcare costs. (source)

Current storyline

Rutinel, the top fundraiser in the Democratic primary, leaned heavily on his family's immigrant story in a district with a large Latino population, winning the nomination over Bird with backing from SEIU Colorado and former Interior Secretary Ken Salazar.

source

None on file

Iowa 1st District

General election 2026-11-03

Cook: Toss Up Crystal Ball: Toss Up
MM

Mariannette Miller-Meeks (R)

Incumbent U.S. Representative

Background

Four-term Republican, physician, and Army veteran; won her past two elections by fewer than 800 votes combined, making her one of the most vulnerable House Republicans in the country. (source)

Current storyline

This will be the third meeting between Miller-Meeks and Bohannan, following two of the closest House races in the country in 2022 and 2024. Miller-Meeks has Trump's endorsement and won her primary by a healthier margin than in past cycles, but Iowa's competitive political environment -- with Democrats also running strong in the state's Senate and 3rd District races -- keeps this race a top national target.

source · source

Endorsements

President Donald Trump
CB

Christina Bohannan (D)

Former Iowa State Representative

Background

Former state representative and law professor making her third consecutive bid against Miller-Meeks after losing by just 799 votes in 2024; running on abortion rights and healthcare affordability. (source)

Current storyline

Bohannan enters her third straight rematch against Miller-Meeks with the closest margin yet behind her -- just 799 votes in 2024 -- and a political environment in Iowa that has shifted meaningfully in Democrats' favor this cycle.

source

Iowa 3rd District

General election 2026-11-03

Cook: Toss Up Crystal Ball: Toss Up
ZN

Zach Nunn (R)

Incumbent U.S. Representative

Background

Second-term Republican, Air Force veteran, and former state legislator; running for re-election in a district anchored in Des Moines after winning by less than 4 points in 2024. (source)

Current storyline

Iowa's 3rd District, anchored in the Des Moines metro, is considered one of the state's most competitive seats alongside the 1st District and the open Senate race. Trone Garriott enters the general election unopposed in her own primary but with a track record of outperforming typical Democratic margins in tough legislative districts, setting up a genuine toss-up against Nunn.

source · source

Endorsements

None on file

ST

Sarah Trone Garriott (D)

Iowa State Senator

Background

State senator who has won a series of competitive races in Republican-leaning legislative districts; ran unopposed in her primary and is positioning herself as a proven vote-getter in tough terrain. (source)

Current storyline

Trone Garriott heads into the general election with a legislative track record of winning in districts Republicans typically hold, a credential her campaign is leaning on heavily against Nunn in this closely-watched Des Moines-area seat.

source

New Jersey 7th District

General election 2026-11-03

Cook: Toss Up Crystal Ball: not yet rated
TK

Tom Kean Jr. (R)

Incumbent U.S. Representative

Background

Two-term Republican whose family has a decades-long political legacy in New Jersey; has been absent from Congress since early March 2026 for treatment of depression, missing more than 140 votes. (source)

Current storyline

Kean's nearly four-month absence from the Capitol -- for which he later disclosed a depression diagnosis -- has become the defining issue of the race, with Bennett repeatedly invoking his absence on the campaign trail. Bennett enters the general election as the well-funded Democratic nominee in a district Trump carried by just 1 point in 2024, one of the tightest partisan splits of any district in the country.

source · source

Endorsements

None on file

RB

Rebecca Bennett (D)

Former Navy Helicopter Pilot

Background

Former Navy helicopter pilot and healthcare executive; won a competitive four-way Democratic primary after centering her campaign on Kean's prolonged absence from Washington. (source)

Current storyline

Bennett defeated three rivals -- including physician Tina Shah, who attacked her over a past Republican registration -- to win the nomination, and has said she'll work to motivate both the Democratic base and center-right Republicans uneasy with Kean's absence.

source

ML

Mike Lawler (R)

Incumbent U.S. Representative

Background

Two-term Republican and one of only three House Republicans representing a district Kamala Harris carried in 2024; seeking a third term after winning his past two races by roughly one point each. (source)

Current storyline

Conley enters the general election as one of the strongest recruits in the country for Democrats, backed by national security-focused groups and a resume built around 16 years of active-duty service. Lawler, one of the top targets in the country given his Harris-won district, has attacked Conley over her prior work at AI firms and her limited time actually living in the district, previewing a contentious fall campaign.

source · source

Endorsements

None on file

CC

Cait Conley (D)

Army Veteran, Former NSC Official

Background

Army veteran who deployed six times to Iraq and Afghanistan and later served on the National Security Council and at the Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency; won a decisive victory in a five-candidate Democratic primary. (source)

Current storyline

Conley won roughly 49% of the vote in a five-candidate field, defeating Rockland County Legislator Beth Davidson and progressive Tarrytown trustee Effie Phillips-Staley, with support from national groups including VoteVets and the Bench.

source

Two-term incumbent representing the Cincinnati area; running for a third term after winning re-election by a wide margin in 2024. (source)

Current storyline

Won re-election by a wide margin in 2024 after flipping the Cincinnati-area seat in 2022; considered increasingly safe for Democrats.

Ohio 9th District

General election 2026-11-03

Cook: Toss Up Crystal Ball: not yet rated
MK

Marcy Kaptur (D)

Incumbent U.S. Representative

Background

The longest-serving woman in congressional history, representing northwest Ohio since 1983; running for re-election in a district Trump carried in 2024 despite her consistent wins. (source)

Current storyline

This is a rematch of 2024's closest House race in Ohio, when Merrin came within roughly 2,300 votes of unseating the 21-term incumbent. Merrin emerged from a crowded five-candidate Republican primary that included a former ICE deputy director and a state legislator, setting up another close contest in one of the 13 House districts nationally that voted for Trump while electing a Democrat in 2024.

source · source

Endorsements

None on file

DM

Derek Merrin (R)

Former Ohio House Speaker

Background

Former Ohio House Speaker who lost the 2024 general election to Kaptur by roughly 2,300 votes; won a five-way Republican primary to secure a rematch. (source)

Current storyline

Merrin defeated four rivals -- including former ICE Deputy Director Madison Sheahan and military veteran Alea Nadeem -- in the May 5 primary, setting up a second consecutive run at the seat Kaptur has held since 1983.

source

Pennsylvania 7th District

General election 2026-11-03

Cook: Toss Up Crystal Ball: Toss-up
RM

Ryan Mackenzie (R)

Incumbent U.S. Representative

Background

Freshman Republican who flipped this Lehigh Valley seat in 2024, unseating two-term Democrat Susan Wild; running for a second term in what's widely seen as one of the purest swing seats in the country. (source)

Current storyline

Democrats view Mackenzie's Lehigh Valley seat as one of their best pickup opportunities nationally, with all six PA-7 primary candidates together raising more than $1.5 million in a single recent quarter -- a sign of how much outside attention the race is drawing. Brooks won the Democratic nomination with Gov. Shapiro's backing over three rivals including a former federal prosecutor and a former county executive.

source · source

Endorsements

None on file

BB

Bob Brooks (D)

Retired Firefighter, Union President

Background

Retired firefighter and president of the Pennsylvania Professional Fire Fighters Association; won a competitive four-way Democratic primary with Gov. Josh Shapiro's endorsement. (source)

Current storyline

Brooks led the four-way primary with about 40% of the vote, ahead of former Northampton County Executive Lamont McClure, former federal prosecutor Ryan Crosswell, and engineer Carol Obando-Derstine.

source

Gov. Josh Shapiro

Pennsylvania 8th District

General election 2026-11-03

Cook: Toss Up Crystal Ball: not yet rated
RB

Rob Bresnahan (R)

Incumbent U.S. Representative

Background

Freshman Republican businessman who flipped this Scranton-area seat in 2024 by defeating longtime Democratic Rep. Matt Cartwright; running for a second term amid scrutiny over his stock trading. (source)

Current storyline

Cognetti enters the race as a well-known local figure after two terms as Scranton's mayor, unopposed in her own primary while Bresnahan faces no Republican challenger either. National Democrats have criticized Bresnahan over his personal stock trading activity while in Congress, an issue Cognetti has already raised on the trail; most forecasters currently lean toward Republicans holding the seat, making it one of the less competitive of Pennsylvania's marquee House races this cycle.

source · source

Endorsements

None on file

PC

Paige Cognetti (D)

Mayor of Scranton

Background

Scranton mayor who won her second full term in 2025; running unopposed in her primary with backing from Gov. Josh Shapiro and EMILY's List. (source)

Current storyline

Cognetti launched her campaign positioning herself as a watchdog on ethics, criticizing Bresnahan directly over his stock trading, and cleared the Democratic field without a primary contest.

source

Pennsylvania 10th District

General election 2026-11-03

Cook: Toss Up Crystal Ball: Toss-up
SP

Scott Perry (R)

Incumbent U.S. Representative

Background

Six-term Republican and former chair of the House Freedom Caucus; won re-election in 2024 by just over a point in a rematch against the same Democratic challenger he'll face again in 2026. (source)

Current storyline

This is a rematch of 2024's razor-thin PA-10 race, which Perry won by just over 5,000 votes. Both candidates are unopposed in their own primaries, meaning the general election campaign effectively began the moment the 2024 results were certified, with Democrats viewing Harrisburg-area PA-10 as one of their best remaining pickup opportunities in the state.

source · source

Endorsements

None on file

JS

Janelle Stelson (D)

Former TV News Anchor

Background

Former television news anchor making her second consecutive bid against Perry after losing by roughly 5,100 votes in 2024; running on lowering costs, protecting Social Security and Medicare, and abortion rights. (source)

Current storyline

Stelson announced her second run in July 2025 and cleared the Democratic primary field, setting up an immediate rematch against Perry in one of the closest House races in the country last cycle.

source

Background

Two-term incumbent, the first Republican to represent this South Texas border district in over a century; running for a third term. (source)

Current storyline

The first Republican to represent this South Texas border district in over a century; running for a third term.

None on file

BP

Bobby Pulido (D)

Tejano Singer, Latin Grammy Winner

Background

Latin Grammy-winning Tejano music star making his first bid for elected office; won the Democratic primary decisively over emergency room physician Ada Cuellar, with national Democrats viewing his fame and socially conservative profile as their best shot at flipping this heavily Latino, Trump-won border district. (source)

Current storyline

A Latin Grammy-winning Tejano music star making his first bid for office, seen by Democrats as their best shot at winning back this heavily Latino border district.

Texas 34th District

General election 2026-11-03

Cook: Lean Republican Crystal Ball: Lean Republican
VG

Vicente Gonzalez (D)

Incumbent U.S. Representative

Background

Third-term Democrat representing this South Texas border district since 2023; previously defeated Republican Mayra Flores in 2022 and 2024, and faces a new GOP challenger after 2025 redistricting made the seat more competitive for Republicans. (source)

Current storyline

Gonzalez has now beaten a Flores in back-to-back elections -- Mayra Flores in 2022 and 2024 -- but faces a new opponent in Eric Flores after 2025's mid-decade Texas redistricting redrew the district's lines to be more favorable to Republicans. Cook Political Report and Crystal Ball currently rate the race Lean Republican, while Inside Elections rates it a Toss-up, reflecting real disagreement among forecasters about how much the new map actually helps the GOP in a district Biden would have carried by double digits under the old lines.

source · source

Endorsements

None on file

EF

Eric Flores (R)

Attorney, Army Veteran

Background

Attorney and Army veteran who defeated Mayra Flores (no relation) in a six-candidate Republican primary; running as Republicans' latest attempt to flip this heavily Hispanic border district. (source)

Current storyline

Flores defeated five rivals including Mayra Flores, who had previously held the seat in a 2022 special election, to win the Republican nomination in the state's first major primary of the cycle.

source
